Article.ArticleTitle: Shielding effect-induced enhancement of chloride ion transport in manganese oxide mixed matrix membranes for efficient separation.

Abstract.AbstractText: Ion-selective membranes play a vital role in sustainable water management and resource recovery. However, conventional polymer and ion-exchange membranes often exhibit limited effectiveness in separating ions with similar physicochemical properties. To address this challenge, we developed a novel mixed matrix membrane composed of sulfonated polyether sulfone (SPES) and hydrated manganese oxide (HMO) NPs for highly efficient and selective chloride ion (Cl-) separation under high-salinity conditions. The SPES matrix contains a high proportion of negatively charged sulfonic acid groups (-SO3H), which act to impede the anion transport of Cl-, H2PO4-, CO32-, SO42-, and other anions through dielectric repulsion. Notably, the uniformly dispersed HMO NPs regulate the interfacial charge environment via a charge shielding effect, thereby enhancing Cl- permeation. Simultaneously, the HMO NPs exhibited pronounced selective adsorption toward CO32-, SO42-, and H2PO4-, enhancing the ion diffusion energy barrier within the SPES@HMO membrane. As a result, the membrane achieved efficient, continuous, and selective separation of Cl⁻/CO32-/SO42-/H2PO4-. By synergistically combining charge modulation and competitive adsorption mechanisms, this innovative membrane design presents a novel strategy for controlled ion separation in water-energy systems, offering a viable solution for advanced water purification and resource recovery applications.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declaration of competing interest The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
